reasoning: A vaccine for the common cold does not currently exist. The common cold is caused by hundreds of different viral strains (primarily rhinoviruses and coronaviruses), making the development of a universal vaccine exceptionally difficult. No advanced clinical trials (Phase 3) for a universal common cold vaccine are currently underway or expected to report results and achieve regulatory approval by late 2025. Furthermore, any addition to the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' would require a major, globally recognized scientific breakthrough and regulatory approval, which cannot occur within the short 1- to 6-month timeframes of the resolution dates. Therefore, the probability relies only on the marginal chance of Wikipedia vandalism or a premature, disputed edit being present on exactly the resolution dates, which remains extremely low. I have assigned the minimum possible probability (0.02) to the near-term dates, increasing marginally to 0.04 for the furthest date to reflect inherently increasing uncertainty over time as per the forecasting guidelines.
